title: Wonderful World
notes: Dan sings lead and strums guitar; Paul sings harmonies and plays lead guitar. Neighborhood friend, Don Triguero, who gave the Volumes their name, introduces the group here. They learned this song from the Herman's Hermits recording.

title: Mr. Tambourine Man
notes: Dan sings lead, strums guitar, and emulates the Byrd's opening bass line; Paul sings harmonies and plays lead guitar.

title: Travellin' Light
notes: Dan sings lead and strums guitar; Paul whistles and plays lead guitar. Don Triguero provides the send-off. This is another song they learned from the Herman's Hermits recording.

title: Satisfaction
notes: Dan sings lead and strums guitar; Paul sings harmonies and plays lead guitar.

title: Getting There
notes: Paul sings lead and plays lead guitar; Dan sings harmonies and strums guitar. This was the first original song they recorded, and probably the first song Paul ever wrote.

title: You Don't Know What It's Like
audio: The Volumes/06 You Don't Know What It's Like.m4a
notes: Dan sings lead and plays guitar; Pete sings harmonies and drums on a folding card table chair. This was the first song Dan ever wrote.

title: Boys
notes: Dan sings lead and plays guitar; Pete sings harmonies and drums on a folding card table chair. They learned this song from the Beatles cover version, of course.

title: Gloria
notes: Paul sings lead and plays lead guitar; Dan sings harmonies and strums guitar; Pete sings harmonies and drums on a folding card table chair.

title: You're Gonna Lose That Girl
audio: The Volumes/09 You're Gonna Lose That Girl.m4a
notes: Paul sings lead and plays lead guitar; Dan sings harmonies and strums guitar; John sings harmonies (in his only known Volumes performance); Pete drums on a folding card table chair.

title: Play With Fire
notes: Paul plays lead guitar; Dan plays fingerpicking guitar. Paul's counterpoint part was inspired by Jorma Kaukonen.

title: Day Tripper
notes: Paul sings lead and plays tambourine; Dan plays lead guitar; neighborhood friend, Dan Degnan, plays rhythm guitar. Pete adds yelling and screaming and general chaos.

title: So Much Better
notes: Dan sings lead; Paul sings background parts and plays lead guitar; Dan Degnan plays rhythm guitar. Pete insists that it's time to eat.

title: She Left Me Last Night
audio: The Volumes/13 She Left Me Last Night.m4a
notes: Paul sings lead on the verses and harmonies on the bridges, and plays lead guitar; Dan sings lead on the bridges and plays rhythm guitar.

title: I've Met a Girl
audio: The Volumes/14 I've Met a Girl.m4a
notes: Paul sings lead and plays lead guitar; Dan plays rhythm guitar.

title: Put Me Off
notes: Paul sings lead and plays lead guitar; Dan plays rhythm guitar.

title: I Think I'm Falling In Love Again
audio: The Volumes/16 I Think I'm Falling In Love Again.m4a
notes: Dan sings lead and plays rhythm guitar; Paul sings backing vocals and plays lead guitar.

title: Day After Day
notes: Dan sings lead on the verses and counterpoint on the last bridge, and plays rhythm guitar; Paul sings harmonies on the verses and plays lead guitar.

title: Go Home to Your Baby
audio: The Volumes/18 Go Home To Your Baby.m4a
notes: Paul sings lead and plays guitar; Dan sings background vocals and plays guitar

title: The Woman Always Gets Her Man
audio: The Volumes/19 The Woman Always Gets Her Man.m4a
notes: Paul sings lead and plays guitar; Dan plays guitar.

title: Hands Off
notes: Paul sings lead and plays lead guitar; Dan sings harmonies and plays rhythm guitar.

title: Photographs
notes: Paul sings lead and plays lead guitar; Dan plays rhythm guitar.

title: Melon Seller
notes: Dan sings lead and plays rhythm guitar; Paul sings harmonies and plays lead guitar.

title: She's In Love With Me
audio: The Volumes/23 She's In Love With Me.m4a
notes: Dan sings lead on the verses and plays rhythm guitar; Paul sings lead on the choruses and plays lead guitar.

title: She Took a Look At You
audio: The Volumes/24 She Took a Look At You.m4a
notes: Paul sing lead and plays lead guitar; Dan plays rhythm guitar.

title: I'm Getting Wiser
notes: Paul sings lead and plays lead guitar; Dan plays rhythm guitar.

title: Even the Angels
audio: A Wake In Time/Even the Other Angels.m4a
notes:
Guitars, MIDI: Piano, Drums.

This is one of the oldest recordings in this collection, and as such, it represents my sound engineering ignorance a bit. But I don't want to mess with it because I think it was exactly what I was going for at the time. I especially like the solo guitar at the end.

I did write some interesting lyrics for this, but a vocal track would have spoiled it.

Even the angels know
In some other sort of time
In some other kind of world than yours and mine....

title: We Win
notes: Guitars, MIDI Drums.

title: True Calling
notes:
Guitars, MIDI: Piano, Double Bass, Drums, Percussion.

Pure instrumental. From the time I invented The Baitnickels, my multitracking, MIDI-influenced persona, I've had an interest in quirky, melodic pieces. This is one.

title: Rabbit and the White Fish
notes:
Vocals, Guitars, Bass Guitar, MIDI Piano.

A bedtime fable for little Julian. I imagine these creatures living undisturbed along the river under the big trees, but you're free, Julian, to illustrate it in your own mind any way you wish.

title: I Knew the Moment
notes:
Vocals, Guitars, MIDI: Piano, Marimba, Vibraphone, Percussion.

I knew the moment I met you there was something special in store for us.

title: Both Sides
audio: A Wake In Time/Both Sides Now.m4a
notes:
Guitars, Bass Guitar, MIDI: Piano, Drums.

I've turned to my old Martin D-18 guitar for my most recent songwriting here. And Zach lent me his Fender P-Bass for a while. The songs are still a little quirky, but I like the organic feel—all the nuances available in vibrating strings.

title: A Wake In Time
audio: A Wake In Time/A Wake in Time.m4a
notes:
Vocals, Guitars, Bass Guitar.

When he was about 8 months old, Julian and I sat alone in the courtyard of our old San Jose house, he in his stroller, I on the front doorstep. Chris and Becca were inside, cleaning out the last of their things so the house could be sold. In one of those timeless moments, Julian and I fixed on each others eyes, feeling out our common roots.

title: Promises We Make
notes:
Vocals, Guitars, Bass Guitar, MIDI: Piano, Glockenspiel.

Written near the beginning of the pandemic, during the acute moments of isolation, dreaming about getting together in the redwoods with people I love.

title: A Meeting of the Minds
notes:
Vocals, Guitars, Bass Guitar, MIDI: Piano, Harp, Drums.

Imagining those private early morning moments between Laurel and Toonie.

In my latest attempts to smooth out the errors in my vocal pitch, I've resorted to a sound engineering trick that I learned from the early Beatles' records--double-tracking vocals. Multiple vocal performances of the same melody can smooth out pitch imperfections. I use very little reverb here, and apply different EQ balances to each of the three voices. For this song, this also helps to create the mood I'm aiming for--something a bit dreamy and spacious.

title: One Is Fine
audio: A Wake In Time/One Is Fine.m4a
notes:
Guitars, Bass Guitar, MIDI: Piano, Harp, Drums.

One is fine they tell me, one is fine
Two is too, too many too much of the time
When you lose your nerve
When you start to fall
One is fine they tell me, one is all....

title: Labor Days
notes:
MIDI: Piano, Double Bass, Harp, Drums.

Just MIDI.

title: Quotations and Sign Posts
notes:
Vocals, Guitar, MIDI: Piano, Double Bass, Harp, Drums.

Talking to myself.

title: Chambers
notes:
Guitars, Tongue Clicks, MIDI: Piano, Double Bass, Harp, Flute, Phasing Bells, Tubular Bells.

More instrumental play. Playing with lots of sound effects. The echoing tongue clicks were serendipitous.

title: All I Know
audio: A Wake In Time/All I Know.m4a
notes:
Vocals, MIDI: Piano, Double Bass, Harp, Drums.

Hiking in the forest—the beginning of a deep relationship.

title: Take It Bring It
audio: A Wake In Time/Take It Bring It.m4a
notes:
Guitars, Bass Guitar, MIDI: Drums.

Playing bass guitar is really fun.

title: So and So
notes: Playing with audio pitch alterations.

title: Limbo
notes: It's possible this could grow up to be a song someday.

title: Hum
notes: Sounds of the 2022 Solano Stroll with lots of audio effects.

title: bass ditty
notes: Little bass ideas.

title: Primates
notes: When the animals run loose....

title: Ah So
notes: Ah, so that's how it is....

title: In and Out
notes: Following the breath.
